content: Just chiming in to say that none of the Ultra Wardens (Winkle, Wanda, Erika) are very good at stacking shields. It would be very very cool if they got some better shield scaling or stats to help their teams survive and do their jobs properly. <:pray:1356053116260454534>
Erica shields for a lot if invested
the best buff she could get would probably be some aliemus gain on x cost like Ogier has on his

<:Aurita_pinch:1468258028271833119> (to be fair it requires a lot of investment but unfortunately most characters in this game require a lot of investment, the bottleneck of investment/growth materials is a separate issue entirely from character balance)

I mean, sure, that's great if you're willing to discard your whole hand at the time (and run an aequor/ultra team for sweet slug). But meanwhile her Defense feels almost useless and the shields gained on her exalt are beaten out by Arachne's exalt by almost 3 times the stat ratio. It just feels low most of the time, and not everyone will have their Winkle at level 80.

That screenshot is without sweet slug, just with full hand. Her exalt does much more than just shielding plus it has fragile dispel so it makes sense the shield on it is lower
And yes, if you want high shields in this game you have to invest in the character, but the issue of investment resources is a different issue from character balance. If you allow a shielder to be strong without much investment, then they become overpowered once someone does invest heavily in them. I do think there's very much a shortage of investment materials which makes investing in defensive shielders a much worse proposition for newer players than investing in a carry who can clear content, but the balance is fine there, it's just a problem of bottlenecked investment materials

I would argue that Arachne doesn't need any investment beyond level 60 and maybe 5/10 soulforge to get 400 shields baseline with her exalt. Arachne's exalt does much more than just shield, as well; it's a potential win condition on its own that bypasses the vast majority of defenses in the game. The fragile dispel is definitely nice on Winkle's, though, you got me there. <:Winkle_frustrated:1468257899330539774>
